description_text: Este é um  Banco de Talentos,  destinado ao cadastro de  Pessoas com Deficiência para todas as unidades da John Deere no Brasil . Se você é uma pessoa com deficiência, fique à vontade para se candidatar a qualquer outra posição disponível em nosso site. Caso não encontre nenhuma vaga que seja de seu interesse, cadastre-se aqui, e avaliaremos suas informações para futuras oportunidades que possam surgir. Seja você, seja John Deere. Faça parte do nosso time! Áreas para as quais recrutamos no Brasil Administração Geral; Contábil; Controladoria; Engenharia; Financeiro; Jurídico; Logística; Marketing; Operações; Order Fulfillment; Qualidade; Recursos Humanos; Saúde, Segurança e Meio Ambiente; Suporte ao produto; Suprimentos; Tecnologia da informação. Unidades John Deere Brasil: São Paulo Campinas Indaiatuba Goiás Catalão Rio Grande do Sul Horizontina Montenegro Quem não é uma pessoa com deficiência, pode se inscrever? Este banco de talentos é destinado a todas as pessoas com deficiência, caso não faça parte desse grupo, acesse nossas oportunidades na  página de carreiras . (Por favor, anexe o seu laudo) Por que a John Deere está fazendo um banco de talentos para pessoas com deficiências? Reconhecemos e valorizamos a pluralidade dos nossos times. Por isso, criamos condições para a equidade de oportunidades através de políticas, práticas e atitudes que promovam uma cultura de respeito e o desenvolvimento de talentos. O Que Você Receberá Na John Deere, você conseguirá desenvolver uma carreira que levará você aonde quer chegar. Além de produzirmos máquinas conectadas e inteligentes, com a mais alta qualidade, criamos soluções e serviços que garantam que nossos clientes possam gerir seus negócios de forma sustentável e produtiva. Com conexões tecnológicas, sociais e humanas, você terá liberdade de explorar novos projetos e pensar de forma inovadora.

Trabalhamos para que a vida possa avançar e estamos sempre à procura de pessoas inovadoras que possam contribuir com a nossa missão. Que tal fazer parte do nosso time? Local Principal: Brasil (BR) - São Paulo - Indaiatuba Função: Product Engineering (CA) Título: Pessoa Engenheira do Produto PL - Indaiatuba/SP - 118728 Suas Responsabilidades Como Pessoa Engenheira do Produto PL, no Centro de Pesquisa e Desenvolvimento da John Deere em Indaiatuba/SP, você atuará no desenvolvimento e melhoria contínua de componentes e sistemas para pulverizadores e plantadeiras, contribuindo diretamente para a evolução tecnológica das soluções agrícolas da John Deere. Principais Responsabilidades: Projetar novos componentes e sistemas ou aprimorar itens existentes para equipamentos agrícolas; Desenvolver e validar soluções envolvendo estruturas, hidráulica, elétrica e conjuntos mecânicos; Criar e atualizar desenhos técnicos, especificações e documentação de engenharia; Conduzir análises técnicas, revisões de design e investigações de falhas; Suportar todo o ciclo de desenvolvimento do produto, desde conceito até validação e produção; Colaborar com equipes multifuncionais (qualidade, manufatura, supply chain, validação etc.); Acompanhar feedback de campo, analisando causas e implementando melhorias; Garantir que as soluções atendam targets de performance, durabilidade, segurança e custo. Habilidades Que Você Precisa Graduação Completa em Engenharia Mecânica, Elétrica, Mecatrônica ou áreas correlatas; Experiência prévia em desenvolvimento de produtos, projetos mecânicos e de sistemas; Conhecimento e experiência com os softwares CREO e Ansys, ou ferramentas similares; Inglês avançado para comunicação com times globais e documentação técnica; Conhecimento em desenvolvimento de sistemas complexos (hidráulicos, elétricos, estruturais); Capacidade de interpretar requisitos, transformar necessidades em soluções e documentar projetos; Boa comunicação, visão colaborativa e habilidade de atuar em times multidisciplinares. O Que Faz Destacar Experiência com máquinas agrícolas, especialmente pulverizadores ou plantadeiras; Vivência com validação, testes e processos de ciclo de vida de produto; Experiência em suporte à manufatura ou a campo; Conhecimento em DFMEA, PFMEA e metodologias de melhoria contínua; Familiaridade com tecnologias de agricultura de precisão: spot spray, taxa variável, section control, JDLink e telemetria; Disponibilidade para viagens (até 25%). Nossos principais benefícios Remuneração variável; Plano de Saúde; Plano Odontológico; Seguro de Vida; Previdência Privada; Benefício Ótica; Reembolso vacina; Universidade Corporativa; TotalPass; Dependendo da unidade, você terá restaurante no local, vale refeição ou alimentação; Auxílio farmácia; Entre outros LI-Hybrid Nosso Processo Seletivo A seleção começa com a sua inscrição, para algumas vagas, existem alguns testes adicionais. Você terá a possibilidade de conhecer pessoas do time nas entrevistas! Nosso processo seletivo é flexível em termos de tempo, então não se preocupe! Nossa equipe de Talent Acquisition está comprometida em proporcionar a melhor experiência para você. Analisamos todas as candidaturas cuidadosamente e fornecemos feedback em todas as etapas do processo. Além disso, todas as orientações serão enviadas por e-mail! Sobre a John Deere A John Deere é líder global no fornecimento de produtos, tecnologia e serviços avançados para clientes que desempenham um papel essencial na agricultura, construção, florestal entre outros setores. Nosso objetivo é atender à crescente necessidade mundial de alimentos, combustível, abrigo e infraestrutura, enquanto promove a sustentabilidade e a preservação do planeta que chamamos de lar. Inovamos para impulsionar a produtividade e proteger nosso planeta e convidamos você a fazer parte dessa história. Trabalharemos juntos para criar, testar, superar para que para que a vida possa avançar. ??

Engage as a technical consultant on mainframe and database technology solutions, supporting complex and mission-critical business requirements in a hybrid environment; Design, develop, deploy, and maintain DB2 for z/OS and enterprise database solutions, ensuring performance, availability, security, and compliance, Partner with application delivery, infrastructure, security, governance, and compliance teams to support mainframe-based and hybrid environments, aligned with JDF's architecture strategy Maintain strong collaboration with application teams to evolve database and mainframe capabilities, including modernization and integration initiatives with cloud platforms, Ensure reusability, efficiency, and stability in the design, build, and support of mainframe and database infrastructure; Perform performance tuning, capacity planning, and cost optimization for DB2 and enterprise database environments; Apply and evolve best practices, standards, and controls for mainframe, database, and hybrid platforms; Support monitoring, troubleshooting, root cause analysis, and prevention of issues in DB2, mainframe, and supporting infrastructure; Evaluate new tools, technologies, and practices related to database management, mainframe modernization, and hybrid architectures (including AWS). Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Systems Analysis and Development, or related fields; Advanced English: broad vocabulary, good grammar, conducts meetings, argues, negotiates, and delivers training sessions; Strong experience analyzing and translating business requirements into database and mainframe technical solutions; Solid experience with DB2 (preferably DB2 for z/OS), including administration, performance tuning, and SQL optimization; Strong mastery of SQL tuning and performance optimization; Experience working in mission-critical, high-availability environments; Understanding of application development environments, data management, and infrastructure constraints in enterprise systems; Strong analytical skills with high attention to detail and accuracy; Ability to provide technical leadership and mentor teams; Experience with AWS (compute, security, storage, etc.) and hybrid cloud integration is important; Availability to work in a hybrid model, 2 times a week, in Indaiatuba/SP. Experience with mainframe modernization initiatives, integrations, or coexistence with cloud platforms; Experience with database migrations involving legacy or enterprise platforms;

Ref. 117237 - Staplerfahrer (m/w/d) NOTHING RUNS LIKE A DEERE! An unserem Standort in Mannheim arbeiten rund 3.500 Mitarbeitende für das größte John Deere Werk Europas. Im Herzen der Rhein-Neckar-Region produzieren wir smarte Traktoren für Kunden in aller Welt. Dabei setzen wir auf innovative Technologien und nachhaltige Lösungen. Auf dem Campus befindet sich neben der Produktion auch das europäische Headquarter. #wirsindmannheim Unsere Abteilung Werkslogistik sucht zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt eine*n Staplerfahrer (m/w/d) Werde Teil eines motivierten, agilen und innovativen Teams das in einem spannenden und internationalen Umfeld arbeitet. Mit Deiner Arbeit trägst Du persönlich zu einer erfolgreichen Produktentwicklung und höchster Kundenzufriedenheit bei. Darauf kannst Du Dich freuen: Modernes Arbeiten in einer 35h/Woche Vollzeit Hervorragende Sozialleistungen, wie eine arbeitgeberfinanzierte Betriebsrente und Optionen steuerbegünstigter privater Altersvorsorge (Entgeltumwandlungspläne, Sonderkonditionen HDI/Allianz, AVWL) Weitere Benefits wie bezuschusstes Deutschlandticket, Company Bike, FamPlus uvm. Ein attraktives Vergütungspaket in Anlehnung an den gültigen Tarifvertrag der IG Metall; darüber hinaus prozentuale Erfolgsbeteiligung und individuelle Leistungszulagen Das Gehalt für diese Position (Vollzeit) basiert auf der Entgeltgruppe 03 des Tarifvertrags der Metall- und Elektroindustrie Baden-Württemberg und setzt sich neben dem monatlichen Bruttoentgelt von ca. 2.870 € durch verschiedene Sonderzahlungen (Urlaubsgeld, T-Zug, Weihnachtsgeld, Trafobaustein) zusammen. So kannst Du in dieser Position ein jährliches Einstiegsgehalt von ca. 39.600,00 EUR erwarten. Diese Aufgaben erwarten Dich: Materialbewegungen mithilfe von Flurförderfahrzeugen Be- und Entladen von Fahrzeugen mithilfe von Gabelstaplern Systemunterstütztes Kommissionieren und Sequenzieren Materialversorgung der Getriebe- und Traktorenmontage Das bringst Du mit: Gabelstaplerführerschein setzen wir voraus, PKW-Führerschein von Vorteil Berufserfahrung im Bereich Logistik von Vorteil Abgeschlossene Ausbildung im Elektro-/Metallgewerbe oder in der Logistik von Vorteil Bereitschaft zur Schichtarbeit Teamfähigkeit, Engagement, Motivation und Flexibilität Teamwork, Professionalität und eine gewisse Hands-On-Mentalität machen Dich in dieser Rolle stark. „We live the team“ ist für uns nicht nur ein Satz, sondern unsere DNA! Wenn Du dich damit identifizieren kannst, dann bist Du bei uns richtig. Diese Position ist zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt zunächst befristet für 12 Monate in Mannheim zu besetzen. Egal wer Du bist - bei John Deere bist Du herzlich willkommen! Wir sind der festen Überzeugung, dass Vielfalt und Einbeziehung uns noch stärker machen. Du findest Dich hier wieder - dann freuen wir uns auf Deine Online-Bewerbung über unser Bewerberportal! Nicht jeder kann von sich behaupten, dass die eigene Arbeit zur Grundversorgung der wachsenden Weltbevölkerung beiträgt. Bei John Deere kannst Du das! Als führender Landtechnikhersteller fertigen wir moderne Land-, Forst-, und Baumaschinen sowie Maschinen für Rasen- und Grundstückspflege. In den letzten Jahren haben wir das Unternehmen zu einem Technologiekonzern entwickelt. Teile mit uns, unsere Vision einer Farm der Zukunft, basierend auf künstlicher Intelligenz, Automatisierung und Konnektivität, um unsere wachsende Welt nachhaltig zu versorgen. #leapforward John Deere Werk Mannheim Talent Acquisition Ronja Knorr Zusätzliche Informationen: Zur Bearbeitung eingehender Bewerbungen nutzen wir ausschließlich unser Online-Tool auf der John Deere Karriereseite. Wir bitten um Verständnis, dass wir Bewerbungen per E-Mail oder Post nicht berücksichtigen können und kein Anspruch auf Rücksendung von Bewerbungsunterlagen besteht. Bei Fragen zu Deiner Bewerbung wende Dich gerne an: RecruitingGermany@JohnDeere.com.

Performs the physical and/or administrative tasks involved in the shipping, receiving, storing, distributing and order fulfillment of merchandise, products, materials, parts, supplies and equipment. Performs quality checks on outbound and inbound shipments. Performs outbound staging and packing in accordance with site policies. Executes ERP system transactions on outbound and inbound shipments. Updates FMS for parcel and freight shipments. Learns to use handheld RF scanners and Warehouse Management System to fulfill orders. Maintains inventory accuracy. Handles and controls hazardous material on both inbound and outbound. Complies with 5S safety, housekeeping practices, and regulations. Completes all required safety training and follows protocol in all risk situations. Uses Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) appropriate to tasks at hand. Lifts up to 75 pounds unassisted. Certification on powered industrial trucks including work assist vehicle (WAV), off the ground order picker (up to 35' off the ground), reach truck, dock stocker and powered pallet jacks. Creates repair RMA's, manages receiving and shipment issues within repair systems. Develops familiarity with a subset of products, processes, operational metrics and software tools; learns and follows basic work instructions for functional assignments according to productivity and quality standards Provides input geared towards continuous improvement, work instruction documentation, and standardization of processes. Resolves a variety of basic problems of limited complexity under direct supervision and performs rudimentary analysis involving a limited number of factors. Creates expense orders for internal repair consumption. Full knowledge of ISO requirements and demonstrated ability to ensure compliance with external auditors. High School Diploma or GED preferred but not required. Basic computer literacy, with knowledge of Microsoft Office. Proficient understanding of the use of a desktop. Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) Required (safety glasses, steel-toed boots, gloves, etc.): Yes Come join the herd! Work for a company that cares, offers a growth environment, and a culture of respect, inclusion and recognition - our front-line workers have an average 7-year tenure! Healthcare, Dental & Vision, Tuition Reimbursement 401k with Company Match Quarterly Cash Incentive Opportunity 20 Days Starting PTO 2 Floating Holidays and Paid Volunteer Time Off Annual Shoe Allowance and free Company Apparel

This is a product design position for a creative and motivated entry level mechanical engineer. In this role, you will contribute to the design, development, and testing of next generation enterprise RFID products, as well as support our current industry-leading product line. This is a hands-on position where you will work alongside senior engineers to bring innovative concepts from the drawing board to full-scale production, helping to solve real-world challenges for our customers. Prior experience via internships, co-ops, and/or a master's degree is highly valued but not required. Collaborate with multi-disciplinary teams to assist in the mechanical design and development of rugged, high-performance RFID products. Work through all phases of product development from concept brainstorming to production release. Create 3D CAD models and detailed 2D drawings using Creo for both new product development and product sustaining activities. Design, analyze, prototype, and test electromechanical parts, subassemblies, and systems. Engage directly with JDMs and suppliers on cost-effective part designs and manufacturing processes for high-volume production. Perform tolerance analyses as well as stress and thermal analyses using hand calculations. Use problem solving skills to troubleshoot customer issues and perform root cause analyses. Propose viable solutions for engineering problems and then execute those solutions in detail, while working within schedule and budget constraints. Document design specifications, test procedures, and results according to Zebra's quality standards. BS degree in Mechanical Engineering and 1+ year experience in Mechanical Engineering Prior experience via internships, co-ops, and/or a master's degree is highly valued but not required. Proficiency using Creo CAD software for part/assembly design and drafting Solid understanding of fundamental mechanical engineering principles Strong communication and teamwork skills

Responsible for all customer and internal facing hardware and software solutions. Assist in developing Windows desktop and command line applications, with potential involvement in API projects using C# and WebAPI under IIS (Specific project not yet determined) Assisting with the design of the application Coding the application Alpha testing the application Creating technical and user documentation Conducting formal and informal training sessions Develop planning, organizational and leadership skills Increase technology knowledge and skills Develop team-based work proficiencies Develop interpersonal skills to connect with day-to-day business contacts through follow up activities Current enrollment in an undergraduate degree program in Computer Science and Engineering or related field. Familiarity with databases, SQL, and stored procedures. Familiarity with Git & Python is preferred Interest in expanding programming skills to include C# and .NET Proficient in Microsoft Office (including Excel, Word, & PowerPoint) Intellectual curiosity with a strong desire to learn and grow Proven leadership and excellence in professional, academic, and/or extracurricular experiences Ability to collaborate as part of a team Effective verbal and written communication skills

Collect and review all payroll-related data, such as timesheets, new hire information and employee changes for your designated payroll. Ensure the data is processed accurately and in timely manner, cooperate with external and internal parties to meet agreed deadlines. Provide documentation for accounting entries and ensure their accuracy. Follow-up on any identified discrepancies. Cooperate with Payroll providers to ensure Tax, Social security and other payments are made and reporting produced accurately and in a timely manner. Ensure that internal controls and processes are followed. Liaise with employees, People team and payroll providers on any payroll issues in coordination with senior team members. Act as an owner of your assigned duties, communicate issues or escalate in timely manner to the senior team members or direct supervisor Backup other Payroll team members when required Maintain strict confidentiality of all records High school diploma is advantage Requires less than 1 year of experience Must be highly organized, detail minded, and able to analyze complex payroll transactions Ability to communicate effectively both oral and written with a customer service approach Ability to work within a team environment and possess skills to support process change Competency in spreadsheet and word processing applications

Communication Skills - Clear and effective verbal and written communication Adaptability and Learning Agility - Willingness to learn quickly, curiosity and need to gain new skills, embrace new tools or methods, and thrive in a dynamic, fast-paced environment. Technical Skills - Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and basic familiarity with technology solution Integrity - Commitment to ethical behaviour and confidentiality. A proactive problem-solving mindset, creative thinking and a passion for logical puzzles is a plus Basic knowledge of scripting language (for example VBA, Python, JavaScript) or query language (Power Query , SQL) is an advantage Concurrent enrolment in a degree-seeking post-secondary educational institution 0 years

Support payroll processing for EMEA region & US Download a report with payroll data for the current month and compare to previous month to check changes Gather any other HR input information for current month Create a brought forward report Provide the input information to the payroll provider Download the payroll output from payroll provider's software Check the payslips and provided overview with the provided input information Discuss with payroll provider possible changes Create Gross to net report for final Manager's review Prepare the salary payment requests Review of provided payroll journal and send to General Ledger team Communicate with the payroll provider and other teams Participate on a payroll project Proficient level of English (B2) Proficiency in MS Excel and Outlook Interest in Finance & HR field Highly organized and detailed personality Good interpersonal skills with a customer service approach and a team spirit

Collaborate with and learn from Senior Designers, Product Managers, and Engineers to generate creative solutions to design problems. Actively participate in and support all stages of the design process, including conducting user research (interviews, surveys) and usability testing. Translate user needs and business requirements into tangible design artifacts, including personas, journey maps, wireframes, and interactive prototypes. Present concepts and research findings to internal teams for feedback, and learn to incorporate that feedback into your work. Contribute to our established design system, ensuring consistency and high-quality execution. Must be a current university student pursuing a degree in HCI, Interaction Design, Product Design, Human Factors, Psychology, or a related field. Your degree program must include a formal "Year in Industry" or "Sandwich Year" placement. You must be scheduled to return to your final year of university studies in September 2027. You must have demonstrated experience and a keen interest in both user research and UX/digital product design. A portfolio demonstrating your design process, problem-solving skills, and examples of both research and design projects (from academia or personal work) is required for consideration. Tools: Academic or personal experience using collaborative design and research tools (e.g., Figma, Miro). Frameworks: A basic understanding of or familiarity with Agile/Scrum development processes. Design Skills: A foundational understanding of interaction design, visual design principles, and design systems. Problem-Solving: Strong conceptual and problem-solving abilities with an eagerness to learn and adapt. Communication: Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the confidence to present your work and ideas.

Analytical Capabilities: Proficiency in analyzing sales data and customer behavior, identifying patterns and areas for improvement. Ability to interpret key performance metrics and present performance reports to clients. Effective Communication: Excellent verbal and written communication skills in both Spanish and English, as Zebra is a global company.Ability to deliver impactful and persuasive presentations. Customer Orientation: A mindset focused on customer needs, always seeking to add value and build long-term relationships. Innovative Mindset: Ability to identify innovative opportunities to help customers improve productivity and efficiency. Good level of English to interact with global teams and international clients. At least 3-5 years of experience in similar roles within the technology or industrial sector. Previous experience working with multinational companies or in high-tech environments. To protect candidates from falling victim to online fraudulent activity involving fake job postings and employment offers, please be aware our recruiters will always connect with you via @zebra.com email accounts. Applications are only accepted through our applicant tracking system and only accept personal identifying information through that system. Our Talent Acquisition team will not ask for you to provide personal identifying information via e-mail or outside of the system. If you are a victim of identity theft contact your local police department. This approach enhances efficiency and promotes fairness in the hiring process. However, every decision regarding interviews and hiring is made by our dedicated team, because we believe people make the best decisions about people. For more on how we use technology in hiring and how we process applicant data, see our Zebra Privacy Policy. Business and Financial Impact - Responsible for individual revenue attainment with established prices and personal expenses Relative Size and Scope - Moderate to average individual quota size for business and like roles Types of Projects - Many transactions with some moderately complex deals Strategic Impact for Zebra - Medium/mid-term Sales and account management certifications (e.g., Certified Sales Professional - CSP). Technical certifications in Zebra solutions or related technologies (IoT, RFID, etc.).
